title: Home – LEMA Lab
text:
Discover the Principles of Intelligence; Embrace the Joy of Research.

Welcome to the LEMA Lab in the Department of Computer Science at the National University of Singapore. We are a collaborative group of researchers working to build intelligent agents that can perceive, reason, and act in the physical world. Our research bridges machine learning, computer vision, and robotics, with a focus on learning agents that can generalize from limited data, adapt during deployment, remember what matters, and improve through experience.

Pronounced “lemma” in English, our lab is committed to discovering simple and scalable principles behind embodied intelligence, with as little manual effort as possible. Read as “乐马” in Mandarin, our lab was born in the Year of the Horse (马), dedicated to fostering the joy (乐) of learning, exploration, and growth in every lab member.

title: Contact – LEMA Lab
text:
Due to the high volume of inquiries, I may not be able to respond to every message. If you believe you are a strong fit and do not receive a reply within one week, you may follow up by email using the subject line “NUS-LEMA Application | [Expected Role] | [Current Affiliation]”.
Our lab has openings for 3–6 PhD students, 1 postdoc fellow, and several visiting students in 2027. Please submit the application form first, including a CV, research interests, and links to representative work.
Application Form




For PhD applicants, please also follow the NUS CS PhD application process by June 15 (spring intake) or Dec 15 (fall intake).



For postdoc applicants, please include a CV, representative papers, reference contacts, and possible project directions.



For internship or visiting applicants, please include an expected timeline, funding status, and research interests.

title: Contact – LEMA Lab
text:
Why Join Us


Lab Research
Our recent work has been recognized with Best Paper awards at ICLR, CVPR, and RSS workshops, Oral at ICML, and invited talks at UC Berkeley, OpenAI, NVIDIA, etc.


Global Collaboration
Our lab builds on broad collaborations across NUS and with researchers at leading institutions worldwide, including Stanford, Harvard, ETHZ, EPFL, MPI, Tsinghua, ISTA, Oxford, and beyond.


NUS AI Research
NUS is consistently recognized among the leading computer science programs, ranked globally at #4 by QS, #5 by US News, and #13 by Times in recent subject rankings.


Singapore AI Hub
Singapore is emerging as a global AI hub, with growing activity from Google DeepMind, Microsoft Research, and other leading industry labs, creating strong opportunities for research careers.

title: Contact – LEMA Lab
text:
Who We Look For


Minimum Qualifications

Uncompromising intellectual honesty and research integrity.
Strong background in machine learning, robot learning, computer vision, robotics, physics, or related areas.
Good written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain ideas clearly and collaborate effectively.



Preferred Qualifications

Intrinsic curiosity and genuine interest in fundamental research.
Independent thinking, ownership, and a demonstrated record of initiative or exceptional achievement.
Evidence of substantial prior research experience, such as high-quality publications or submissions in relevant venues.




Email: liuyj [at] comp.nus.edu.sg
Address: COM 1, 13 Computing Drive, Singapore 117417


© 2026 LEMA Lab. All rights reserved.

title: EMC2 Laboratory
text:
Research Directions
We study how embodied agents can learn from interaction, reason about the physical world, and improve autonomously.


Adaptive Robot Policies
How can robot policies adapt dynamically at inference time to handle new tasks, environments, and embodiment variations?


Interactive World Models
How can structured world models help agents anticipate spatial, temporal, and physical interactions before acting?


Proactive Self-Improvement
How can agents verify and learn from their own predictions to improve continually with minimal human supervision?

title: EMC2 Laboratory
text:
Welcome

Welcome to the EMC2 Lab at UNC Charlotte. We are interested in understanding, designing, and optimizing multi-phase, mutli-scale, thermochemically reacting systems to enable next-generation energy, propulsion, and critical materials technologies. We utilize both experimental and modeling approaches, ranging from lab-scale tests and quantitative diagnostics to computer-assitsted theoretical modeling, to reveal fundmental physcial and chemical mechanisms controlling key process in these systems.

title: EMC2 Laboratory
text:
